chunk_summary: This section focuses on responsive feeding skills and structured mealtime practices to support texture progression in complementary feeding for children aged 6–24 months, preventing feeding delays and promoting healthy eating habits.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of responsive feeding in promoting healthy eating behaviors in children aged 6–24 months.
Learn strategies for introducing appropriate food textures to prevent feeding delays.
Develop structured mealtime practices to support child development and feeding skills.
key_facts: Responsive feeding involves recognizing and responding to a child's hunger and satiety cues during meals.
Introducing lumpier textures by 8–10 months can help prevent feeding delays and promote oral motor skill development.
Structured mealtime routines improve feeding outcomes and reduce mealtime stress for both caregivers and children.
WHO recommends starting complementary feeding at 6 months while continuing breastfeeding.
UNICEF emphasizes the role of caregiver interaction in fostering positive feeding experiences.
reference_quote: Responsive feeding is a key component of complementary feeding, requiring caregivers to be attentive to a child’s hunger and satiety cues while providing age-appropriate food textures. (UNICEF, Responsive Feeding Information Sheet)
